title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins Golden Globes Best Screenplay Award
text: Paul Thomas Anderson claimed the Best Screenplay prize at the 2026 Golden Globes for his film "One Battle After Another," an adaptation of Thomas Pynchon's novel "Vineland." During his acceptance, Anderson described writers as "magpies" who "steal all the bits and pieces that everybody says." He credited multiple sources for lines featured in the screenplay. Anderson acknowledged castmember Shayna McHayle (stage name Junglepussy) for contributing dialogue for her character J.P., stating: "Shayna McHayle's a great writer. I stole a line from her." He also referenced borrowing from Pynchon's source material and drawing inspiration from a Nina Simone quote about freedom. Anderson's win came against notable competitors including Ronald Bronstein and Josh Safdie for "Marty Supreme," Ryan Coogler for "Sinners," and Chloé Zhao and Maggie O'Farrell for "Hamnet." The ceremony took place January 11, 2026, at the Beverly Hilton, hosted by Nikki Glaser and broadcast on CBS and Paramount+ With Showtime.
